topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The afternoon sun slanted across the worn wooden desk, illuminating dust motes dancing in the air. I was halfway through sketching a particularly frustrating architectural detail when I noticed it. My journal, usually tucked securely under my textbook, was open on my nightstand. The page visible was a dense collection of looping script—my handwriting, but somehow… altered. A faint tremor ran through my hand as I leaned closer, my breath catching in my throat. It wasn't just open; it was actively being read. My gaze flickered towards the doorway, where Liam was sprawled on his bed, ostensibly watching a video, but his eyes were fixed on the page with an unnerving stillness. I slowly closed the book, my heart thumping a rapid, uneven rhythm against my ribs. When he finally looked up, his expression was unreadable, a blank slate that made the hairs on my arms stand up. "Everything alright?" he asked, his voice flat, devoid of warmth. I just swallowed, the dryness in my mouth making the simple response feel monumental.

[story 2]

It started subtly. I’d left my diary open on my desk one evening, a record of anxieties and fleeting observations about campus life. When I returned the next day, the ink looked… different. Not smudged, but subtly rearranged. A section detailing my fears about finals week had been underlined, not with a pen stroke, but with something darker, almost like a fingertip smudge. I picked up the journal, my fingers tracing the unfamiliar markings. I looked over at Chloe, who was sitting at the shared table, engrossed in her laptop, completely oblivious. I tried to rationalize it—maybe I’d misread it, maybe it was just a trick of the light. But the feeling of being observed was persistent, a cold pressure building behind my sternum. I finally looked up, meeting Chloe’s eyes for a fraction of a second. Her gaze slid past me, lingering for a beat too long on the journal sitting innocently beside my coffee mug, before returning to her screen. I picked up the book and slid it under my pillow, the fabric feeling suddenly too thin, and retreated to the safety of my room.

[story 3]

The silence in the dorm room had become heavy, thick enough to choke on. I was trying to focus on my chemistry notes, but every few minutes, my peripheral vision would snag on the desk. My journal, a sanctuary of my unfiltered thoughts, was open. I didn't have the nerve to look directly, so I kept my eyes fixed on the textbook, but I could feel the weight of someone’s attention pressing down on me. A faint rustling sound drew my attention. Liam was sitting in his chair, ostensibly studying, but his posture was rigid, his head tilted just so, his eyes locked onto the page. My internal monologue stuttered. *What is he doing?* I couldn't move. My hands felt clammy, and I found myself gripping my pen so tightly the plastic creaked. When he finally shifted, a barely perceptible movement, I could see the shadow of his thumb brushing across a line of my writing. A wave of sheer bewilderment washed over me, leaving a strange, hollow sensation in my chest. I slowly lowered my hand, my gaze darting between the journal and his face, searching for some explanation that simply didn't materialize.
